mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0 percent, severe labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which forces equity repricing downward over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC below 0.01, or single-series effects completely dominate the joint signal in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, positive month-over-month changes in industrial production signal rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during recovery phases with a signal-weighted portfolio Sharpe ratio below 0.5, or the relationship reverses during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates positively correlate with aggregate corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling 3-year windows, yielding a Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the twelve-month inflation rate implied by CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 percent, anticipated central bank tightening compresses equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation with an ICIR below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates signals labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict 5-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.01, or the signal is statistically indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, decelerating industrial production signals weakening economic momentum that reduces equity risk premiums, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal performs equally well in early-cycle phases, invalidating the regime dependency.
